name: ExtensionMedication_TotalVolume
title: R5: When the specified product code does not infer a package size, this is the specific amount of drug in the product additional types
description: R5: `Medication.totalVolume` additional types (Quantity) additional types from child elements (code, comparator, system, unit, value)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Medication.totalVolume` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Medication.totalVolume` 0..1 `Quantity`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Medication.totalVolume` 0..1 `Quantity`
*  R4B: `Medication.amount` 0..1 `Ratio`
*  R4: `Medication.amount` 0..1 `Ratio`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Medication.totalVolume` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Medication.amount` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Medication.totalVolume` do not cover the following types: Quantity.
The mappings for `Medication.totalVolume` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: code, comparator, system, unit, value.

element.short: When the specified product code does not infer a package size, this is the specific amount of drug in the product
element.definition: When the specified product code does not infer a package size, this is the specific amount of drug in the product.  For example, when specifying a product that has the same strength (For example, Insulin glargine 100 unit per mL solution for injection), this attribute provides additional clarification of the package amount (For example, 3 mL, 10mL, etc.).
